description: AKOÚŌ v0.6 example: a memory-lineage listening output with the new instrumentation fields (apparatus, listener, memory, per-claim source and time_range), plus a /remember routing plan carrying budget and preset_id.

listening_output.object_listened_to: Harbor field recording, fourth in a two-year series

known_blind_spots: Mono 16 kHz input: no stereo-image claims and no content claims above roughly 8 kHz.
No absolute playback or capture level is known.
capture_notes: Recorder model unknown for two of the consulted records.

memory.lineage_note: Fourth listening in the harbor series; recurrence of machinery keynote, thinning bird activity across records.

heard.statement: A low continuous machinery tone with intermittent high calls is present in the current recording.
heard.basis: Host caption pass over the supplied audio

measured.statement: The current file is 120.0 seconds, mono, 16 kHz, with energy concentration below 500 Hz.

inferred.statement: The three consulted records and the current listening plausibly form one series of the same harbor position.
inferred.basis: Matching store tags and recurring machinery-tone claims across consulted records

interpreted.statement: Across the series, bird-activity claims thin while machinery claims persist, reading as increasing mechanical dominance at this site.
interpreted.basis: Comparison of stored claims over two years; apparatus varied between records

undetermined.statement: Whether apparent change reflects the site, season, recorder position, or apparatus differences remains undetermined; one consulted record lacks capture notes.

what_appears: The current listening can be situated against three stored records without letting memory overwrite present description.
A lineage note and record identifier are ready for registration into the store.
what_remains_hidden: Species identity, exact machinery source, and any change in microphone position across the series remain unavailable.
The store's coverage outside the queried tags is unknown.

technical: Mono 16 kHz capture bounds what the model pass can hear.
spatial: Recorder distance and position shape which harbor layers become audible.
archival: The store's curation and query scope decide which past listenings are available for comparison.
computational: Model caption and DSP features are distinct evidence streams and are marked per claim.

hallucination: Do not invent stored records or lineage links beyond the three consulted identifiers.
over_identification: Similarity between records must not become identity of source or place.
forensic_overreach: The series comparison is curatorial, not evidentiary.
source_confusion: Memory-derived claims are marked source: memory and never enter heard or measured for the present sound.

listening_output.main_reading: The recording reads as a recurrence within a known series: stable machinery keynote, thinning bird activity, with apparatus variation as an unresolved confound.
listening_output.alternative_reading: A signal-inspection pass over all four recordings under matched settings could test whether the apparent thinning is a capture artifact.

routing_plan.object_listened_to: Harbor field recording, fourth in a two-year series

mode_chain.reason: The task is recurrence and registration against a stored series.
Fresh perceptual description must precede memory comparison.
Apparent change across records could be apparatus change and needs measurement.

agent_handoff.summary: Run the remember preset: memory-lineage primary over three consulted records, acoulogical grounding first, signal corrective for apparatus confounds; register the listening with the prepared lineage note.
required_inputs: record identifier from the host store
forbidden_assumptions: Do not treat store similarity as source identity.
Do not treat absence from the store as novelty in the world.
Do not read apparatus-driven change as site change.

stop_conditions: Stop before memory or lineage claims if the store or its query scope becomes unavailable.
Stop before registration if the host does not assign a record identifier.
